name: slack__remote-mcp
description: Official Slack remote MCP server. Search messages, files, channels, and users; send and draft messages; read threads; manage reactions and canvases. OAuth 2.0.
instructions:
**âš ď¸Ź Enable your Slack app for MCP access first.** Open the app's **App Assistant** settings (`https://api.slack.com/apps/<APP_ID>/app-assistant`) and turn it on. Until you do, connecting fails with `App is not enabled for Slack MCP server access` â€” even after OAuth succeeds.

**Requires your own Slack app** â€” the Slack MCP server only accepts OAuth from a directory-published or internal Slack app with a fixed app ID; dynamic client registration and unlisted apps are rejected. A workspace admin may also need to approve the app before it can connect.

1. Create a Slack app at https://api.slack.com/apps (or reuse an existing internal app).
2. Open the app's **App Assistant** page and enable it for MCP access.
3. Under **OAuth & Permissions**, add the user-token scopes for the tools you need:
   - Search â€” `search:read.public`, `search:read.private`, `search:read.files`, `search:read.users`
   - Read channels & threads â€” `channels:history`, `groups:history`, `im:history`, `mpim:history`, `channels:read`
   - Send & react â€” `chat:write`, `reactions:read`, `reactions:write`
   - Canvases â€” `canvases:read`, `canvases:write`
   - Users, files & emoji â€” `users:read`, `users:read.email`, `files:read`, `emoji:read`
4. Add this redirect URL to the app: `http://localhost:3000/oauth-callback`
5. Install the app to your workspace, then paste the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** into this form.

Endpoints (auto-discovered from the server URL; set manually only if discovery is blocked):
- Authorization Endpoint â€” `https://slack.com/oauth/v2_user/authorize`
- Token Endpoint â€” `https://slack.com/api/oauth.v2.user.access`

Docs: https://docs.slack.dev/ai/slack-mcp-server/

tools.name: slack_search_public_and_private
tools.description: Search public and private messages and files

tools.description: Read the replies in a thread

tools.description: Read the contents of a file

tools.description: List the members of a channel
